text:
Market today: U.S. markets are CLOSED today for the Juneteenth holiday - they reopen Monday. Nothing will trade until then, so here is where you stand and what to watch.

Where things stand: Stocks ended last week strong. Thursday (6/18) the Nasdaq jumped about 1.9% and the S&P rose about 1.1%, led by a big bounce in chip and tech stocks - exactly the kind of names you own. Two things drove the good mood: the U.S.-Iran conflict cooled off (the U.S. ended its blockade and a peace deal is in place), which calmed oil and nerves, and fresh optimism about U.S. chipmaking (Intel jumped about 9% on a reported Intel-Apple chip partnership).

One caution from the Fed: at Tuesday's meeting (6/17) the new chair Kevin Warsh left rates unchanged but signaled a possible rate HIKE later this year and took rate cuts off the table. Higher-for-longer rates are a mild headwind for pricier growth names - worth keeping in mind for PLTR, SNOW, CRWD and QQQ.

Your holdings: Your book is chip-and-tech heavy (AVGO, SMH, QQQ, SPY, PLTR, SNOW, CRWD), so last week's chip rebound was a tailwind. Broadcom (AVGO, your biggest position) is the bumpy one - its AI business is growing fast (AI chip revenue up triple-digits) but a light forward guidance earlier this month caused a sharp drop, and it is still your weakest spot (down around 13%). Watch whether it holds and recovers with the group. SpaceX (SPCX) is still a brand-new, very volatile IPO - manage it by hand. Everything else looks fine.

Bottom line: Nothing to do today (market closed). Mood is risk-on into Monday thanks to calmer geopolitics and a chip rebound, but the Fed leaning toward a hike is the one cloud - do not chase, and keep an eye on Broadcom.
